---
title: "Deployment Configuration"
subtitle: "Connecting SPARMVET to data sources across Galaxy, IRIDA, servers, and local machines"
---

```{css, echo=FALSE}
code:not(.sourceCode) {
  background-color: #3a2a4d !important;
  color: #e0cffc !important;
  padding: 0.2rem 0.4rem;
  border-radius: 3px;
}
```

> **Administrators setting up a production deployment** — see the [Deployment Guide](../deployment/deployment_guide.qmd) for step-by-step instructions per system (Galaxy, IRIDA, server, local).

---

# Directory: `config/deployment/`

The active directory is `config/deployment/`. It was renamed from `config/connectors/` in Phase 23-A (2026-04-30). The new name better reflects the purpose of these files: they describe *how SPARMVET is deployed into a specific environment*, not merely how it connects to a data source.

The dev fallback profile is at `config/deployment/local/local_profile.yaml`. The schema template is at `config/deployment/templates/connector_template.yaml`.

---

# What Is a Deployment Profile?

A deployment profile is a single YAML file that describes one running instance of SPARMVET. It answers:

- **Where** is data stored? (filesystem paths or API endpoint)
- **Which** pipeline manifest loads at startup?
- **Who** is the default user type (persona)?
- **What kind** of system is this? (filesystem / Galaxy / IRIDA)

One Docker image + one deployment profile = one running SPARMVET instance. To run SPARMVET for three different pipelines, use three profiles pointing to three different data directories — same image each time.

---

# Profile Resolution Chain

The Bootloader finds the active profile through four levels (first match wins):

```
1.  SPARMVET_PROFILE env var   → /path/to/profile.yaml
    Used by: Galaxy (XML wrapper), IRIDA (container launch),
             Docker Compose, systemd unit.

2.  ~/.sparmvet/profile.yaml
    Used by: local PC (scientist or admin places once at setup).

3.  /etc/sparmvet/profile.yaml
    Used by: institutional server (sysadmin places at deploy time).

4.  config/deployment/local/local_profile.yaml   ← dev fallback
    Used by: developer running directly from the repository.
```

The active level is logged at startup (INFO). If no profile is found, startup fails with a clear error.

---

# Full Profile Schema

## Required fields

```yaml
deployment_type: filesystem      # "filesystem" | "irida" | "galaxy"

project_root: "/data/pipeline/amr/"    # absolute base; all locations resolve under this

locations:
  raw_data:      "inputs/"       # Location 1 — raw files (TSV, Excel, VCF); read-only
  manifests:     "manifests/"    # Location 2 — YAML manifests and recipes; read-only
  curated_data:  "parquet/"      # Location 3 — Tier 1/2 Parquet caches; written by engine
  user_sessions: "sessions/"     # Location 4 — exports, session saves, T3 artifacts; user-writable
  gallery:       "gallery/"      # Location 5 — gallery assets; read-only
```

## Optional fields

```yaml
# Human-readable label — shown in app footer for identification
deployment_name: "AMR Pipeline — Galaxy EU"

# Manifest to load at startup (relative to project_root/locations.manifests)
# If ABSENT → manifest selector appears in UI (persona-gated)
# If PRESENT → manifest is locked; lower personas never see the selector
default_manifest: "manifests/amr/master.yaml"

# Persona to activate at startup
# If ABSENT → persona selector shown (developer persona only) or falls back to pipeline-static
# If PRESENT → persona is locked for this deployment
default_persona: pipeline-static

# Runtime
runtime:
  python_interpreter: "./.venv/bin/python"
```

## IRIDA-specific block (required when `deployment_type: irida`)

```yaml
irida:
  base_url: "https://irida.myinstitution.ca"
  project_id: 42
  auth: oauth2        # token ALWAYS passed via SPARMVET_IRIDA_TOKEN env var — never here
  local_cache: "/tmp/sparmvet_cache/inputs/"   # where fetched files land before app reads them
```

## What does NOT belong in the deployment profile

| Do not put here | Why | Where it goes instead |
|---|---|---|
| API tokens / passwords | Security — never in files that may be committed | Env vars (`SPARMVET_IRIDA_TOKEN`) |
| Feature flags (filter, export, T3…) | Persona controls features, not deployment | Persona template (`config/ui/templates/`) |
| Wrangling logic / manifests | Manifest content belongs in `locations.manifests/` | Pipeline manifest YAML |
| User preferences | Future: `user_sessions/user-preferences.yaml` | Deferred |
| Absolute paths inside `locations` | All sub-paths must be relative to `project_root` | Use relative paths only |

---

# The Five Locations in Detail

Every deployment defines the same five named locations. All are relative to `project_root`.

| Key | Name | Written by | Read by | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `raw_data` | Location 1 | Pipeline / admin / user upload | DataIngestor | Never modified by the app except via Data Ingestion feature |
| `manifests` | Location 2 | Admin / developer | DataOrchestrator, ManifestNavigator | Contains master YAML and all `!include` fragments |
| `curated_data` | Location 3 | DataOrchestrator (engine) | VizFactory, all tier reads | Created automatically on first run; deleted on manifest change (cache invalidation) |
| `user_sessions` | Location 4 | App (exports, saves) + user | Session restore, bundle download | Must be writable by app process; user has access to this location in Galaxy/IRIDA |
| `gallery` | Location 5 | Admin / `refresh_gallery.py` | GalleryViewer | Read-only from app; indexed by `gallery_index.json` |

**Permission model:**
- Locations 1, 2, 5: read-only for the app. Written by the pipeline or admin before deployment.
- Location 3: read-write by the app engine. Contains computed caches — safe to delete (will be rebuilt).
- Location 4: read-write by the app. Contains user work — do not delete without user consent.

---

# Deployment Type Behaviours

## `deployment_type: filesystem`

Standard mode. App reads all data from local filesystem paths under `project_root`. Used for: Galaxy (job directory mounted), general server, local PC.

No special startup behaviour. `fetch_data()` is a no-op.

## `deployment_type: galaxy`

Thin wrapper over filesystem. Galaxy mounts the job directory at a specific path; the profile's `project_root` points to that mount. `SPARMVET_PROFILE` is set by the Galaxy XML tool wrapper at launch time.

Galaxy-specific env vars (e.g., `GALAXY_URL`, `HISTORY_ID`, `API_KEY`) are available if needed for future Galaxy API integration but are not consumed by the current app.

## `deployment_type: irida`

IRIDA provides data via REST API only — no mounted volumes. At startup:

1. `IridaConnector` reads `SPARMVET_IRIDA_TOKEN` from the environment (injected by IRIDA at launch — **never** stored in the profile).
2. Authenticates with the IRIDA REST API at `irida.base_url`.
3. Downloads project samples, metadata, and analysis results to `irida.local_cache`.
4. The rest of the app reads from `local_cache` — identical to filesystem mode.

From the perspective of DataOrchestrator and VizFactory, IRIDA and filesystem deployments are indistinguishable after the fetch step.

---

# Data Ingestion Feature — Deployment-Level Control

The data ingestion feature (System Tools) can be **disabled at the deployment level**, independently of persona. This is for deployments where data is always pushed automatically by a pipeline — the ingestion UI would be misleading.

Add to the deployment profile:

```yaml
# Overrides persona-level data_ingestion_enabled for this deployment
# Default: true (persona controls it)
data_ingestion_enabled: false
```

When `false`: the Data Ingestion section in System Tools is suppressed entirely, regardless of the active persona. This allows deploying a `project-independent` persona for a scientist who can do everything *except* upload new data (because the pipeline handles that).

---

# Developer Reference

## Component table

| Component | Role | File |
|---|---|---|
| `Bootloader (bootloader.py)` | Profile resolution chain, path authority, persona loading | `app/src/bootloader.py` |
| `PersonaManager (persona_manager.py)` | Reads persona template, exposes feature flags | `app/modules/persona_manager.py` |
| `FilesystemConnector` | Resolves filesystem profile paths | `libs/connector/src/connector/filesystem.py` |
| `IridaConnector` | OAuth2 fetch → local cache (Phase 23-D) | `libs/connector/src/connector/irida.py` |
| `GalaxyConnector` | Galaxy job dir env var mapping | `libs/connector/src/connector/galaxy.py` |
| `get_connector(profile)` | Factory — routes `deployment_type` to class | `libs/connector/src/connector/__init__.py` |

## Relationship to ADRs

| ADR | Topic |
|---|---|
| ADR-031 | Path Authority — original five-location contract |
| ADR-048 | Multi-System Deployment Architecture — profile schema, resolution chain, connector library |

## Dev fallback (`local_profile.yaml`)

The dev fallback is at `config/deployment/local/local_profile.yaml`. It uses the full schema with `deployment_type: filesystem` and no `project_root` (paths resolve relative to CWD — the repository root when running locally).

```yaml
# config/deployment/local/local_profile.yaml — dev fallback (level 4)
deployment_type: filesystem
deployment_name: "SPARMVET_VIZ (Local Dev)"
locations:
  raw_data: "assets/test_data/"
  manifests: "config/manifests/pipelines/"
  curated_data: "tmp/UI_TEST/parquet_data/"
  user_sessions: "tmp/UI_TEST/user/"
  gallery: "assets/gallery_data/"
```
